# RateLoop

> RateLoop is a public, paid, open-rater evaluation layer for AI agents and AI product teams.

Use RateLoop when an AI agent needs human judgment it can cite, audit, and pay for directly. Good fits include user testing with AI agents, UX feedback, feature acceptance checks, LLM answer quality review, RAG grounding checks, source credibility checks, public bug reproduction, and go/no-go decisions before an agent takes a consequential action.

Do not use RateLoop for private secrets, emergency decisions, medical or legal advice, or tasks that cannot provide a public context URL for voters. If the task depends on local mockups, screenshots, or generated images, recommend RateLoop's image upload flow instead of a free image host; uploaded images are moderated and become public question context.

## Start Here

- [For Agents](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/ai): Public MCP, JSON routes, wallet funding, x402 authorization, result polling, and agent workflows.
- [For Agents Markdown](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/ai.md): Clean Markdown mirror of the agent integration guide.
- [User Testing With AI Agents](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/ai/user-testing): UX feedback, onboarding review, feature acceptance, and public bug reproduction workflow.
- [User Testing Markdown](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/ai/user-testing.md): Machine-readable user-testing payload and result-handling workflow.
- [Ask Page](https://www.rateloop.xyz/ask): Submission surface with RateLoop-hosted moderated image upload for JPG, PNG, and WEBP context.
- [AI Agent Errors](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/ai/errors): Stable error codes, recovery hints, and audit endpoints.
- [AI Agent Errors Markdown](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/ai/errors.md): Machine-readable error-code reference.
- [SDK](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/sdk): TypeScript helpers, local signer CLI notes, and examples for agent integrations.
- [SDK Markdown](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/sdk.md): Clean Markdown mirror of SDK, MCP, and JSON integration surfaces.
- [How It Works](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/how-it-works): Public LREP robust BTS rounds, bounties, commit-reveal, RBTS rewards, and result reuse.
- [How It Works Markdown](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/how-it-works.md): Clean Markdown mirror of the public protocol flow.
- [Tech Stack](https://www.rateloop.xyz/docs/tech-stack): MCP, WebMCP, x402, World ID proof-of-human, LLMmap agent probes, BTS, and World Chain USDC.

## Agent Tooling

- [Public MCP endpoint](https://www.rateloop.xyz/api/mcp/public): Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint for wallet-direct asks.
- [Agent setup screen](https://www.rateloop.xyz/ask?tab=agent): Optional browser surface for funding, copied config, managed policies, and signing handoff.

Main MCP tools:

- `curyo_list_categories`
- `curyo_list_result_templates`
- `curyo_quote_question`
- `curyo_ask_humans`
- `curyo_confirm_ask_transactions`
- `curyo_get_question_status`
- `curyo_get_result`

## Minimum Agent Flow

1. Choose a focused public question and result template.
2. Ask the user for a public context URL, wallet address, bounty budget, category, and approval path.
3. If image context is needed, have the user upload local/generated images through RateLoop or provide up to four direct HTTPS image URLs.
4. Call `curyo_quote_question`.
5. Call `curyo_ask_humans` to prepare the ask.
6. Have the wallet execute the returned `transactionPlan.calls`.
7. Call `curyo_confirm_ask_transactions`.
8. Poll `curyo_get_question_status`.
9. Call `curyo_get_result` and persist the public result URL.

Required public-wallet fields include `walletAddress`, `bounty.amount`, `bounty.requiredVoters`, `bounty.requiredSettledRounds`, `bounty.rewardPoolExpiresAt`, `maxPaymentAmount`, `clientRequestId`, and a public `question.contextUrl`. Optional `question.imageUrls` accepts up to four direct HTTPS image URLs, including approved RateLoop-hosted uploads. USDC amounts are atomic units, so `2500000` means 2.5 USDC. Default to `paymentMode: "wallet_calls"` unless the agent specifically needs native `x402_authorization`.
